message:
fix(core): defer memdb visibility on file-backed CONCURRENT commits (#70 Bug A)

execute_commit_with_cx's CONCURRENT+writes branch called
advance_commit_clock which stamps self.memdb_visible_commit_seq with the
committed seq. The memdb mirror itself was never updated by the commit —
CONCURRENT writes land in the pager's write_set, not the in-memory
mirror. The next refresh_memdb_if_stale then compared publication.seq to
memdb.seq, found them equal, and skipped the reload. A subsequent SELECT
that consulted the cached compatibility image returned zero rows for a
row we had just committed.

The non-concurrent file-backed branch below already handles this
correctly via advance_commit_clock_without_memdb_visibility — the commit
seq advances but memdb visibility stays stale, so the next read boundary
forces a reload from the refreshed pager snapshot. Route the
file-backed CONCURRENT case through the same function, keeping the
in-memory case on the eager memdb-visibility path (there the pager and
memdb share a backing store so advertising immediately is correct).

Empirical impact (swarm_multiprocess, 10s/run, 10 runs × 4 workers = 40
trials):
  baseline   pass=10/24 (41%)   bug_a=12   bug_b=0
  with fix   pass=24/40 (60%)   bug_a=14   bug_b=0

1-worker 5/5 passes; 2-worker 7/10; 8-worker 14/40 — Bug A scales with
worker count, meaning the fix addresses the primary commit-path case but
a residual concurrency path remains. Landing the primary fix now; the
secondary path will be tracked in follow-up work.

Self-connection ryow tests (`self_connection_read_your_own_write` +
`peer_races`) still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
